episodes.title: Le métro, ça me saoule
episodes.topic: Complaining about this morning's métro — packed, delayed, someone's music too loud

lines.french_street: T'as vu ce matin ? Le métro, c'était l'enfer.
lines.english: Did you see this morning? The metro was hell.

lines.french_street: Bah j'étais dedans moi aussi. La ligne 13, bloquée vingt minutes.
lines.english: Yeah I was in it too. Line 13, stuck for twenty minutes.

lines.french_street: Exactement. Et en plus y'avait un mec avec sa musique à fond.
lines.english: Exactly. And on top of that there was a guy blasting his music.

lines.french_street: T'as rien dit ?
lines.english: You didn't say anything?

lines.french_street: J'voulais, mais il avait l'air chaud. Du coup j'ai rien dit.
lines.english: I wanted to, but he looked like trouble. So I didn't say anything.

lines.french_street: Chais pas, moi j'aurais mis mes écouteurs.
lines.english: I dunno, me I would've put my headphones in.

lines.french_street: J'les avais oubliés à la maison, c'est ça le pire.
lines.english: I'd left them at home, that's the worst part.

lines.french_street: Ah non, c'est horrible. On prend le vélo demain ?
lines.english: Oh no, that's awful. Shall we take the bike tomorrow?

lines.french_street: Carrément. Le métro le matin, j'en peux plus.
lines.english: Absolutely. The metro in the morning, I can't take it anymore.

questions.question: Why was the metro particularly bad this morning?
options: There was an accident on the tracks
Line 13 was delayed for twenty minutes

questions.question: Why didn't Inès tell the guy to turn his music down?
options: She thought he looked like trouble
She got off at the next stop
A metro employee already warned him

questions.question: What do Inès and Karim decide to do tomorrow?
options: Leave earlier to avoid the crowd

episodes.title: Deux cafés

lines.french_street: T'as envie de quoi ?
lines.english: What do you feel like?

lines.french_street: Un café, j'crois. T'as vu le prix ?
lines.english: A coffee, I think. Did you see the price?

lines.french_street: Deux euros cinquante, c'est pas cher.
lines.english: Two fifty, that's not expensive.

lines.french_street: Ok. Moi j'prends un crème alors.
lines.english: OK. I'll have a white coffee then.

lines.french_street: Moi un express. On commande et on trouve une table ?
lines.english: I'll have an espresso. We order and grab a table?

lines.french_street: Ouais, y'a de la place au fond.
lines.english: Yeah, there's room at the back.

lines.french_street: Parfait, on y va.
lines.english: Perfect, let's go.

lines.french_street: Ouais, enfin ! Y'a du soleil pour une fois.

lines.french_street: T'as envie d'aller au parc ?
lines.english: Do you feel like going to the park?

lines.french_street: Bonne idée. On y va quand ?
lines.english: Good idea. When do we go?

lines.french_street: Là, maintenant. J'suis libre.
lines.english: Right now. I'm free.

lines.french_street: Parfait, j'prends mon vélo.
lines.english: Perfect, I'll grab my bike.

lines.french_street: Ouais, moi aussi. Bon, on va au marché samedi matin ?
lines.english: Yeah, me too. Well, shall we go to the market Saturday morning?

lines.french_street: Samedi matin... c'est pas trop tôt ?
lines.english: Saturday morning... isn't that too early?

lines.french_street: On y va à onze heures, c'est bon.

lines.french_street: D'accord. Et après ?

lines.french_street: J'vois Camille l'après-midi. T'as qu'à venir.
lines.english: I'm seeing Camille in the afternoon. You can just come along.

lines.french_street: Ouais, pourquoi pas !

lines.english: Where are you? I can't see you.

lines.english: I'm in front of the Voltaire entrance.

episodes.title: Samedi soir
episodes.topic: Planning Saturday night — whose place, who brings what, who to invite

lines.french_street: On fait quoi samedi soir ? T'as des idées ?
lines.english: What are we doing Saturday night? You got any ideas?

lines.french_street: Bah, on pourrait se faire un truc chez moi, mes parents sont pas là.
lines.english: Well, we could do something at mine, my parents aren't home.

lines.french_street: Trop bien. On invite qui ? Lucas et Inès ?
lines.english: Perfect. Who do we invite? Lucas and Inès?

lines.french_street: Lucas ouais, mais Inès j'sais pas — elle s'est engueulée avec Lucas la semaine dernière.
lines.english: Lucas yeah, but Inès I'm not sure — she had a fight with Lucas last week.

lines.french_street: Ah ouais c'est vrai, chais pas si c'est une bonne idée alors.
lines.english: Oh yeah that's true, not sure that's a great idea then.

lines.french_street: On invite juste Lucas du coup. T'as qu'à amener des chips et des boissons.
lines.english: We'll just invite Lucas then. You can bring chips and drinks.

lines.french_street: Ok, j'peux passer au Franprix avant. T'as besoin d'autre chose ?
lines.english: OK, I can stop by Franprix beforehand. You need anything else?

lines.french_street: Non c'est bon, j'ai déjà des pizzas surgelées. On se retrouve vers 20h ?
lines.english: No it's fine, I already have frozen pizzas. We meet around 8?

lines.french_street: Parfait, j'dis à Lucas.
lines.english: Perfect, I'll tell Lucas.

episodes.title: Le nouveau boulot
episodes.topic: First week at a new job — the team, the hours, the open space

lines.french_street: Alors, c'était comment la première semaine ?
lines.english: So, how was the first week?

lines.french_street: Bah, sympa. L'équipe est cool, mais l'open space… c'est vraiment bruyant.
lines.english: Well, nice. The team's cool, but the open space… it's really loud.

lines.french_street: T'as des horaires fixes au moins ?
lines.english: Do you at least have fixed hours?

lines.french_street: En fait non, c'est flexible. J'arrive quand je veux entre huit et dix.
lines.english: Actually no, it's flexible. I can show up whenever between eight and ten.

lines.french_street: C'est pas mal ça.
lines.english: That's not bad.

lines.french_street: Ouais, mais du coup j'suis resté jusqu'à sept heures hier soir.
lines.english: Yeah, but because of that I stayed until seven yesterday evening.

lines.french_street: Ah ouais, quand même. Et t'entends bien avec ton chef ?
lines.english: Oh wow, that's a lot. And do you get on well with your boss?

lines.french_street: Lui il est sympa, mais j'le vois presque jamais, il est tout le temps en réunion.
lines.english: He's nice, but I barely see him — he's always in meetings.

lines.french_street: Du coup t'es un peu livré à toi-même ?
lines.english: So you're kind of left to figure things out on your own?

lines.french_street: Exactement. Mais bon, chais pas, j'préfère ça en fait.
lines.english: Exactly. But honestly, I don't know, I actually prefer it that way.

episodes.title: La résa
episodes.topic: A phone call to book a table for four — nothing at 8pm, negotiating the time

lines.french_street: Allô, bonjour, j'voudrais réserver une table pour quatre ce soir.
lines.english: Hello, hi, I'd like to book a table for four tonight.

lines.french_street: Ce soir… t'as pensé à quelle heure ?
lines.english: Tonight… what time were you thinking?

lines.french_street: Vers vingt heures si c'est possible.
lines.english: Around eight if possible.

lines.french_street: Ah, vingt heures c'est complet, j'ai plus rien.
lines.english: Oh, eight o'clock is fully booked, I've got nothing left.

lines.french_street: D'accord… et vingt-deux heures, y'a quelque chose ?
lines.english: OK… and at ten, is there anything?

lines.french_street: Vingt-deux heures c'est bon, j'ai une table en terrasse.
lines.english: Ten o'clock works, I have a table on the terrace.

lines.french_street: Bah en fait on préfère être à l'intérieur si possible.
lines.english: Actually we'd rather be inside if possible.

lines.french_street: Du coup j'peux vous mettre à vingt et une heures trente, à l'intérieur.
lines.english: In that case I can put you down for nine-thirty, inside.

lines.french_street: Parfait, c'est au nom de Manon.
lines.english: Perfect, it's under the name Manon.

lines.french_street: C'est noté, à ce soir !
lines.english: Got it, see you tonight!

questions.question: How many people is the reservation for?
Why can't Manon get a table at 8pm?
options: They don't take reservations that late

questions.question: What time is the reservation finally made for?

episodes.title: Les soldes
episodes.topic: Back from the sales — what they found, what was a ripoff

lines.french_street: T'as fait les soldes ce week-end ?
lines.english: Did you hit the sales this weekend?

lines.french_street: Ouais, samedi matin. C'était blindé mais j'ai trouvé des trucs.
lines.english: Yeah, Saturday morning. It was packed but I found some stuff.

lines.french_street: T'as pris quoi ?
lines.english: What did you get?

lines.french_street: Une veste en cuir, moitié prix. Par contre les baskets que je voulais, y'avait plus ma taille.
lines.english: A leather jacket, half price. But the sneakers I wanted, they didn't have my size anymore.

lines.french_street: Dommage. Moi j'suis allée en ligne, j'ai commandé un manteau.
lines.english: That's a shame. I went online, I ordered a coat.

lines.french_street: C'était bien réduit ?
lines.english: Was it a good discount?

lines.french_street: Soi-disant moins quarante pour cent, mais en fait le prix de base était gonflé. Arnaque totale.
lines.english: Supposedly forty percent off, but actually the original price was inflated. Total rip-off.

lines.french_street: Classique. T'as annulé la commande ?
lines.english: Classic. Did you cancel the order?

lines.french_street: Ouais, direct. J'vais attendre les vraies promos.
lines.english: Yeah, straight away. I'm going to wait for real deals.

questions.question: What did Max successfully buy at the sales?
Why was Léa's online deal a rip-off?
options: The original price had been inflated
Delivery cost more than the discount

questions.question: What does Léa decide to do in the end?
options: Cancel the order and wait for real deals
Ask Max to return the jacket

episodes.title: Trop crevé
episodes.topic: One of them overslept, missed the alarm, ran for the bus — recounting the morning disaster

lines.french_street: T'as une tête de zombie ce matin.
lines.english: You look like a zombie this morning.

lines.french_street: J'ai raté mon réveil, j'me suis levé à huit heures et demie.
lines.english: I missed my alarm, I got up at eight thirty.

lines.french_street: Sérieux ? T'avais pas cours à neuf heures ?
lines.english: Seriously? Didn't you have class at nine?

lines.french_street: Si, du coup j'ai même pas pris de café, j'ai couru jusqu'à l'arrêt.
lines.english: Yeah, so I didn't even have coffee, I ran all the way to the bus stop.

lines.french_street: T'as chopé le bus au moins ?
lines.english: Did you at least catch the bus?

lines.french_street: Non, j'l'ai raté de trente secondes. J'ai vu les portes se fermer devant moi.
lines.english: No, I missed it by thirty seconds. I watched the doors close right in front of me.

lines.french_street: Oh c'est nul. T'as fait quoi alors ?
lines.english: Oh that's awful. So what did you do?

lines.french_street: J'ai appelé Mehdi, il m'a ramené en voiture. J'étais quand même en retard de vingt minutes.
lines.english: I called Mehdi, he drove me. I was still twenty minutes late.

lines.french_street: Le prof a rien dit ?
lines.english: The teacher didn't say anything?

lines.french_street: Si, mais chais pas, j'étais trop crevé pour m'en souvenir.
lines.english: Yeah, but I don't know, I was too exhausted to even remember.

questions.question: What time did Lucas wake up?
How did Lucas eventually get to class?
options: A friend named Mehdi drove him

questions.question: How late was Lucas when he arrived?

episodes.title: Le voisin relou
episodes.topic: The upstairs neighbor made noise until 3am again — what to do about it

lines.french_street: T'as encore entendu le voisin du dessus cette nuit ?
lines.english: Did you hear the upstairs neighbor again last night?

lines.french_street: Jusqu'à 3h du mat, ouais. J'ai rien pu dormir.
lines.english: Until 3 in the morning, yeah. I couldn't sleep at all.

lines.french_street: C'est la troisième fois cette semaine, c'est pas normal.
lines.english: That's the third time this week, it's not okay.

lines.french_street: J'sais pas, j'ose pas lui dire en face.
lines.english: I don't know, I don't dare say it to his face.

lines.french_street: Bah t'as qu'à glisser un mot sous sa porte alors.
lines.english: Well just slip a note under his door then.

lines.french_street: Et si ça change rien, on fait quoi ?
lines.english: And if that changes nothing, what do we do?

lines.french_street: Du coup on va voir le gardien, y'a forcément un règlement.
lines.english: Then we go see the caretaker — there's definitely a rule about it.

lines.french_street: En fait t'as raison, j'commence par le mot ce soir.
lines.english: Actually you're right, I'll start with the note tonight.

questions.question: How late did the neighbor make noise?
How many times has this happened this week?

options: Leave a note under the door
Talk to the neighbor face to face

episodes.title: Les vacances
episodes.topic: Where to go on holiday — beach vs mountains, budget, dates

lines.french_street: T'as pensé à où on part cet été ?
lines.english: Have you thought about where we're going this summer?

lines.french_street: Bah j'hésite encore — la mer ou la montagne.
lines.english: Well I'm still torn — the beach or the mountains.

lines.french_street: Moi j'veux la mer, j'en peux plus de la chaleur ici.
lines.english: I want the beach, I can't take the heat here anymore.

lines.french_street: Ouais mais la mer en août c'est blindé de monde.
lines.english: Yeah but the beach in August is packed with people.

lines.french_street: Du coup on part fin juillet ?
lines.english: So we leave at the end of July then?

lines.french_street: Ça marche, mais faut qu'on reste dans les 500 euros chacun.
lines.english: That works, but we need to stay within 500 euros each.

lines.french_street: J'ai trouvé un appart à Sète pour 400, y'a deux chambres.

lines.french_street: Sète c'est bien, j'y suis allé gamin. On prend ?

lines.french_street: Ouais, je réserve ce soir.
lines.english: Yeah, I'll book it tonight.

episodes.title: La grève
episodes.topic: Strike day: how each of them is getting to work, bikes, crowded buses, working from home

lines.french_street: T'as vu, encore une grève aujourd'hui…
lines.english: Did you see, another strike today…

lines.french_street: Ouais, bah j'suis venu à vélo du coup. Toi t'as fait comment ?
lines.english: Yeah, well I came by bike then. How did you get here?

lines.french_street: J'ai pris le bus, c'était l'enfer. Bondé à mort.
lines.english: I took the bus, it was a nightmare. Packed solid.

lines.french_street: Ah ouais genre impossible de bouger ?
lines.english: Oh yeah like impossible to move?

lines.french_street: Carrément. J'ai mis une heure pour trois stations, c'est nul.
lines.english: Totally. It took me an hour for three stops, it's ridiculous.

lines.french_street: Bah moi le vélo c'était parfait, chais pas pourquoi j'le fais pas tout le temps.
lines.english: Well for me the bike was perfect, I dunno why I don't do it all the time.

lines.french_street: Ouais enfin toi t'habites à dix minutes hein.
lines.english: Yeah but you only live ten minutes away though.

lines.french_street: C'est vrai, c'est vrai. Et Camille elle a fait quoi ?
lines.english: True, true. And what did Camille do?

lines.french_street: Elle a télétravaillé, la veinarde.
lines.english: She worked from home, lucky her.

lines.french_street: Bah honnêtement on devrait faire pareil la prochaine fois.
lines.english: Honestly we should do the same next time.

lines.french_street: Ouais, bah faut le dire à la chef avant, elle est pas trop pour le télétravail.
lines.english: Yeah, well we need to tell the boss in advance, she's not really into working from home.

lines.french_street: Bref, la prochaine grève je reprends le vélo, moi.
lines.english: Anyway, next strike I'm taking the bike again.

questions.question: How did Thomas get to work during the strike?

episodes.title: T'as vu Julie ?
episodes.topic: Gossip: Julie has a new boyfriend, met on an app, moving fast — opinions differ

lines.french_street: T'as vu Julie ? Elle sort avec un mec qu'elle a rencontré sur une appli.
lines.english: Did you see Julie? She's going out with a guy she met on an app.

lines.french_street: Ouais j'sais, ça fait genre deux semaines non ?
lines.english: Yeah I know, it's been like two weeks, right?

lines.french_street: Même pas, et là il emménage chez elle ce weekend.
lines.english: Not even that, and now he's moving in with her this weekend.

lines.french_street: Quoi ?! Sérieux ? C'est un peu rapide quand même.
lines.english: What?! Seriously? That's a bit fast though.

lines.french_street: C'est ce que je lui ai dit, elle veut rien entendre.
lines.english: That's what I told her, she doesn't want to hear it.

lines.french_street: Bah du coup t'as rencontré le mec au moins ?
lines.english: So have you at least met the guy?

lines.french_street: Non, même pas. Elle dit qu'on va tous se voir samedi.
lines.english: No, not even. She says we're all going to meet Saturday.

lines.french_street: Bref on verra bien. T'y vas toi ?
lines.english: Well, we'll see. Are you going?

lines.french_street: Chais pas encore, ça dépend. Toi ?
lines.english: Not sure yet, depends. You?

lines.french_street: J'vais y aller ouais, faut bien se faire une idée du gars.
lines.english: I'll go yeah, gotta get a sense of the guy.

lines.french_street: Ok on y va ensemble alors.
lines.english: Okay let's go together then.

episodes.title: L'appart
episodes.topic: Apartment hunting: tiny places, huge rents, a visit that went badly

lines.french_street: T'as vu l'appart hier, alors ?
lines.english: So did you see the apartment yesterday?

lines.french_street: Ouais… c'était un cauchemar. Genre, douze mètres carrés pour 850 balles.
lines.english: Yeah… it was a nightmare. Like, twelve square metres for 850 euros.

lines.french_street: 850 ? Sans les charges ?
lines.english: 850? Not including bills?

lines.french_street: Sans les charges, ouais. Et encore, le proprio était même pas là à l'heure.
lines.english: Without bills, yeah. And on top of that, the landlord wasn't even there on time.

lines.french_street: Sérieux… t'as attendu combien de temps ?
lines.english: Seriously… how long did you wait?

lines.french_street: Genre vingt minutes dans le couloir. Bah, du coup j'suis parti.
lines.english: Like twenty minutes in the hallway. So I just left.

lines.french_street: T'as bien fait. C'est pas une façon de traiter les gens.
lines.english: Good call. That's no way to treat people.

lines.french_street: Bref, chais pas quoi faire. Y'a rien d'autre dans mes prix.
lines.english: Anyway, I have no idea what to do. There's nothing else in my budget.

lines.french_street: Mon cousin loue une chambre, faut que j'lui demande si c'est toujours dispo.
lines.english: My cousin rents out a room — I need to ask him if it's still available.

lines.french_street: Ah ouais ? C'est où ?
lines.english: Oh really? Where is it?

lines.french_street: Montreuil. C'est pas le centre mais c'est correct, quoi.
lines.english: Montreuil. It's not central but it's decent enough.

lines.french_street: Si c'est abordable, j'suis preneur. Demande-lui !
lines.english: If it's affordable, I'm interested. Ask him!

questions.question: How much was the rent for the apartment Karim visited?
Why did Karim leave without seeing the apartment?
options: He waited twenty minutes and the landlord still wasn't there
The landlord never answered his calls

questions.question: What does Manon suggest at the end of the conversation?
options: That Karim look on a rental website
That she ask her cousin about a room he rents out
That Karim move to the suburbs alone
That her cousin is looking for a flatmate in Montreuil

episodes.title: Le resto d'hier
episodes.topic: Debrief of last night's restaurant — great starter, slow service, overpriced wine

lines.french_street: Alors, ce resto hier soir, t'as aimé ?
lines.english: So, that restaurant last night — did you like it?

lines.french_street: Bah, l'entrée était top, le carpaccio vraiment bien.
lines.english: Well, the starter was great, the carpaccio was really good.

lines.french_street: Ouais, mais après on a attendu genre une heure pour le plat.
lines.english: Yeah, but then we waited like an hour for the main.

lines.french_street: Une heure, c'est pas normal ça. Le serveur était où ?
lines.english: An hour, that's not normal. Where was the waiter?

lines.french_street: Chais pas, il passait jamais. Du coup j'étais vraiment énervée.
lines.english: No idea, he never came round. I was really annoyed by that point.

lines.french_street: Et le vin, t'as vu le prix ? Trente euros la bouteille, c'était abusé.
lines.english: And the wine — did you see the price? Thirty euros a bottle, that was a rip-off.

lines.french_street: Complètement. Pour du vin ordinaire en plus.
lines.english: Totally. For a really ordinary wine on top of it.

lines.french_street: Bref, c'est pas un endroit où on retourne.
lines.english: Basically, it's not a place we're going back to.

lines.french_street: Non. Y'a le nouveau bistrot rue Lepic, on essaie ça la prochaine fois ?
lines.english: No. There's that new bistro on rue Lepic — shall we try that next time?

lines.french_street: Ouais, bonne idée. J'ai entendu que c'est pas cher et le service est sympa.
lines.english: Yeah, good idea. I heard it's cheap and the service is friendly.

lines.french_street: Parfait, on s'organise ça.
lines.english: Perfect, let's sort that out.

questions.question: What did they think of the starter?
How much did the bottle of wine cost?
What do they decide to do next time?
options: Go back to the same restaurant
Try a new bistro on rue Lepic

episodes.title: Coup de fil au proprio
episodes.topic: Calling the landlord about a leak under the sink — he's evasive, tenant insists

lines.french_street: Allo, c'est Emma. T'as vu mes messages ? Y'a une fuite sous l'évier, c'est urgent.
lines.english: Hello, it's Emma. Did you see my messages? There's a leak under the sink, it's urgent.

lines.french_street: Ouais ouais, j'ai vu. Bah écoute, j'suis super chargé là, j'peux pas venir cette semaine.
lines.english: Yeah yeah, I saw. Look, I'm super busy right now, I can't come this week.

lines.french_street: Cette semaine ?! C'est pas une option, le parquet est déjà tout gonflé.
lines.english: This week?! That's not an option, the floorboards are already all swollen.

lines.french_street: Le parquet… bah genre ça peut pas attendre lundi ?
lines.english: The floorboards… like, can it really not wait until Monday?

lines.french_street: On est vendredi, c'est ça ton plan ? Moi j'ai mis une bassine sous l'évier, ça tient pas.
lines.english: It's Friday, that's your plan? I put a basin under the sink, it's not holding.

lines.french_street: Ok, bah t'as qu'à appeler un plombier toi-même, du coup on règle ça après.
lines.english: OK, well just call a plumber yourself then, we'll sort it out after.

lines.french_street: Hors de question, c'est à toi de le faire. J'te rappelle que c'est dans le bail.
lines.english: No way, that's on you. Let me remind you it's in the lease.

lines.french_street: Bref… ok j'appelle mon plombier ce soir, il peut p'têtre passer demain matin.
lines.english: Fine… OK I'll call my plumber tonight, he might be able to come tomorrow morning.

lines.french_street: Demain matin, t'es sûr ? Parce que j'attends pas plus longtemps.
lines.english: Tomorrow morning, are you sure? Because I'm not waiting any longer.

lines.french_street: Ouais c'est bon, j'te tiens au courant ce soir.
lines.english: Yeah alright, I'll keep you posted tonight.

lines.french_street: Ok. Mais si j'ai rien d'ici ce soir, j'appelle directement un plombier et je déduis la facture du loyer.
lines.english: OK. But if I hear nothing by tonight, I'll call a plumber myself and deduct the bill from the rent.

questions.question: What physical damage has the leak already caused?
What does the landlord first suggest Emma do?
What does Emma threaten if she hears nothing by tonight?
options: She will deduct a plumber's bill from the rent
She will stop paying rent entirely
She will call the building manager

episodes.title: Le match
episodes.topic: Recap of last night's match — the missed penalty, the ref, the last-minute goal

lines.french_street: T'as vu le match hier soir ?
lines.english: Did you watch the match last night?

lines.french_street: Ouais, enfin... j'ai failli éteindre à la mi-temps tellement c'était nul.
lines.english: Yeah, well... I nearly switched off at half-time, it was that bad.

lines.french_street: Bah le penalty raté de Dembélé, chais pas comment t'as tenu après ça.
lines.english: Man, Dembélé's missed penalty — I don't know how you stayed watching after that.

lines.french_street: Il a tiré direct sur le gardien, c'était honteux.
lines.english: He shot it straight at the keeper, it was embarrassing.

lines.french_street: Et l'arbitre ! T'as vu la décision sur le hors-jeu ? Genre il voyait rien.
lines.english: And the ref! Did you see that offside call? Like he couldn't see a thing.

lines.french_street: Ouais y'a eu trois décisions pourries, bref il a gâché le match.
lines.english: Yeah there were three terrible calls, basically he ruined the match.

lines.french_street: Du coup j'allais me coucher et là... but à la 93e !
lines.english: So I was about to go to bed and then... goal in the 93rd minute!

lines.french_street: J'suis tombé du canapé, j'te jure.
lines.english: I fell off the sofa, I swear.

lines.french_street: Moi j'ai réveillé toute la rue, ma coloc était furieuse.
lines.english: I woke up the whole street, my flatmate was furious.

lines.french_street: Ça valait le coup quand même, non ?
lines.english: Still worth it though, right?

lines.french_street: Clairement. On se retrouve samedi pour le prochain ?
lines.english: Definitely. Shall we meet up for the next one on Saturday?

lines.french_street: Chez moi, j'ai enfin le grand écran.
lines.english: At mine — I finally got the big screen.

questions.question: What did Dembélé do during the penalty?
options: He scored but it was disallowed
He shot straight at the goalkeeper

questions.question: When did the decisive goal go in?
Where do they plan to watch the next match?

episodes.title: La soirée de Karim
episodes.topic: Party debrief: who came, who didn't, what happened in the kitchen at 2am

lines.french_street: Alors, la soirée de Karim c'était comment ?
lines.english: So, how was Karim's party?

lines.french_street: Bah, sympa, mais y'avait pas grand monde au début.
lines.english: Well, nice, but there weren't many people at first.

lines.french_street: Sara est venue au moins ?
lines.english: Did Sara come at least?

lines.french_street: Non, elle est venue pas — enfin, elle a annulé genre à la dernière minute.
lines.english: No, she didn't come — I mean, she cancelled like at the last minute.

lines.french_street: Encore ! C'est sa spécialité ça.
lines.english: Again! That's her specialty.

lines.french_street: Ouais bref. Par contre Mehdi et ses potes ont débarqué vers minuit, du coup ça s'est animé.
lines.english: Yeah whatever. But Mehdi and his crew showed up around midnight, so it picked up.

lines.french_street: Et il s'est passé quoi dans la cuisine, j'ai vu des stories chelous ?
lines.english: And what happened in the kitchen, I saw some weird stories?

lines.french_street: Ah ça — vers deux heures quelqu'un a renversé toute la sangria sur Karim.
lines.english: Oh that — around two someone spilled the whole sangria on Karim.

lines.french_street: Non ! C'était qui ?
lines.english: No way! Who was it?

lines.french_street: Chais pas, un mec que je connais pas. Karim était furax mais bon il a rien dit.
lines.english: I don't know, some guy I didn't know. Karim was fuming but he didn't say anything.

lines.french_street: Pauvre Karim. T'aurais dû m'appeler, j'aurais bien vu ça.
lines.english: Poor Karim. You should've called me, I would've loved to see that.

lines.french_street: T'étais où toi alors ?
lines.english: Where were you then?

lines.french_street: Coincée chez ma mère, j'pouvais pas partir avant minuit.
lines.english: Stuck at my mum's, I couldn't leave before midnight.

questions.question: Why didn't Sara come to the party?
options: She was stuck at her mother's place
She cancelled at the last minute

questions.question: What happened in the kitchen around 2am?
options: Karim started a fight with a guest
Someone broke a bottle of wine
Mehdi and his friends caused a scene
Someone spilled all the sangria on Karim

questions.question: Why wasn't Léa at the party?
options: She was stuck at her mother's until midnight
She didn't get the invitation in time

episodes.title: Portable perdu
episodes.topic: One of them lost their phone — retracing steps, the bar, the taxi, find-my-phone

lines.french_street: Max, t'as pas vu mon portable ? J'le retrouve plus.
lines.english: Max, have you seen my phone? I can't find it anymore.

lines.french_street: Bah chais pas moi, t'étais où en dernier ?
lines.english: I dunno, where were you last?

lines.french_street: On était au bar chez Rémi, et après j'ai pris un taxi.
lines.english: We were at Rémi's bar, and then I took a taxi.

lines.french_street: T'as appelé le taxi pour demander ?
lines.english: Did you call the taxi company to ask?

lines.french_street: J'peux pas, mon portable c'est lui le problème.
lines.english: I can't, my phone is the whole problem.

lines.french_street: Bah utilise le mien, attends.
lines.english: Use mine then, hold on.

lines.french_street: Du coup j'me souviens même plus quelle appli j'avais pour le localiser.
lines.english: And now I can't even remember which app I had to track it.

lines.french_street: T'as un iPhone ? Y'a Localiser, c'est intégré.
lines.english: You've got an iPhone? There's Find My, it's built in.

lines.french_street: Ouais, vas-y connecte-toi sur icloud.com avec mes identifiants.
lines.english: Yeah, go ahead and log into icloud.com with my login.

lines.french_street: Ok… bah il est au bar, genre il bouge pas, il est sûrement là-bas.
lines.english: OK… right, it's at the bar — it's not moving, it's definitely still there.

lines.french_street: Trop bien, j'y fonce. Merci Max !
lines.english: Amazing, I'm heading straight there. Thanks Max!

questions.question: Where does Manon think she last had her phone?
Why can't Manon call the taxi company herself?
According to the Find My app, where is the phone located?

episodes.title: Grosse galère
episodes.topic: An epic commute disaster: stuck between stations, no announcements, the bus replacement chaos

lines.french_street: Karim, t'es au courant de la galère ce matin sur la ligne B ? J'suis restée bloquée entre deux stations genre vingt minutes, moteur coupé, le noir complet.
lines.english: Karim, did you hear about the mess this morning on line B? I was stuck between two stations for like twenty minutes, engine cut, total darkness.

lines.french_street: Sérieux ?! Vingt minutes dans le tunnel ? Carrément, j'aurais pété un câble. Et y'avait des annonces au moins ?
lines.english: Seriously?! Twenty minutes in the tunnel? I would've totally lost it. And were there at least any announcements?

lines.french_street: Rien du tout. Le conducteur il disait absolument rien, les gens commençaient à se lever, à taper dans les portes... c'était le bazar total.
lines.english: Nothing at all. The driver wasn't saying anything, people started getting up, banging on the doors... it was absolute chaos.

lines.french_street: N'importe quoi. Et après ils t'ont évacuée à pied dans le tunnel ou quoi ?
lines.english: That's ridiculous. And after that did they evacuate you on foot through the tunnel or what?

lines.french_street: À pied ouais, avec une lampe torche pour tout le monde. On marchait en file indienne sur le ballast, les gens en talons, les enfants qui pleuraient... un film catastrophe.
lines.english: On foot yeah, with one flashlight for everyone. We were walking single file on the track bed, people in heels, kids crying... a total disaster movie.

lines.french_street: Grave, ça me saoule juste d'imaginer. Et une fois sorti du tunnel, t'as pu prendre quoi ?
lines.english: Honestly, it stresses me out just imagining it. And once you got out of the tunnel, what could you take?

lines.french_street: Soi-disant y'avait un bus de remplacement mais personne savait où il s'arrêtait. J'ai attendu trente minutes sur un trottoir, aucun agent, aucun panneau.
lines.english: Apparently there was a replacement bus but nobody knew where it stopped. I waited thirty minutes on a sidewalk, no staff, no signs.

lines.french_street: Chais pas comment la SNCF s'en sort à chaque fois sans que ça parte en scandale national. C'est dingue.
lines.english: I don't know how SNCF gets away with it every time without it becoming a national scandal. It's crazy.

lines.french_street: Bah là y'a des gens qui filmaient, ça tourne déjà sur les réseaux. Apparemment un câble électrique a lâché au-dessus des voies, donc voilà.
lines.english: Well now people were filming, it's already going around on social media. Apparently an electrical cable snapped above the tracks, so there you go.

lines.french_street: Un câble ? Et ils connaissaient pas l'état des infrastructures avant ? Ça me dépasse vraiment. T'as eu quand même un remboursement ?
lines.english: A cable? And they didn't know the state of the infrastructure beforehand? It's just beyond me. Did you at least get a refund?

lines.french_street: J'ai rempli le formulaire en ligne mais j'ai reçu un mail automatique qui dit que le traitement peut prendre jusqu'à trente jours. Trente jours pour un billet à cinq euros, c'est un peu fort.
lines.english: I filled in the online form but I got an automated email saying processing can take up to thirty days. Thirty days for a five-euro ticket, that's a bit much.

lines.french_street: Carrément. Et du coup t'es arrivée à quelle heure au taf finalement ?
lines.english: Totally. And so what time did you end up getting to work in the end?

lines.french_street: Avec deux heures de retard. Mon chef était plutôt compréhensif mais bon, j'avais une réunion à neuf heures que j'ai ratée complètement.
lines.english: Two hours late. My boss was pretty understanding but still, I had a nine o'clock meeting that I missed entirely.

lines.french_street: Putain, deux heures... bon la prochaine fois on covoiture, j'te jure. Je passe te chercher, c'est réglé.
lines.english: Damn, two hours... right, next time we carpool, I'm telling you. I'll come pick you up, that's settled.

questions.question: Why was the train stopped in the tunnel?
options: An electrical cable snapped above the tracks
Maintenance workers were on the line

questions.question: How long did Inès wait for the replacement bus?
What does Karim propose at the end of the conversation?
options: To file a complaint together with SNCF
To work from home next time
To take an earlier train in future
To carpool and pick Inès up

episodes.title: Le date
episodes.topic: Fast gossip debrief of last night's date — the good, the cringe, will there be a second one

lines.french_street: Alors ce date hier soir, t'as survécu ou pas ? Parce que t'avais l'air tellement stressée avant.
lines.english: So that date last night, did you survive or not? Because you looked so stressed beforehand.

lines.french_street: Pff, chais pas trop comment te dire ça... C'était un peu le mélange des deux, quoi. Le restau était top, genre vraiment sympa, mais lui il a passé genre la moitié du repas à me parler de son ex.
lines.english: Pfff, I don't really know how to put it... It was kind of a mix of both, you know. The restaurant was great, like really nice, but he spent like half the meal talking to me about his ex.

lines.french_street: Non ! Mais t'es sérieuse là ? Dès le premier date ?
lines.english: No way! Are you serious? On the very first date?

lines.french_street: Carrément, et en mode... il me décrivait leurs vacances en Grèce ensemble, leurs petites habitudes le matin... j'étais là genre, mais pourquoi tu me racontes ça à moi ?
lines.english: Totally, and like... he was describing their holidays in Greece together, their little morning routines... I was just sitting there like, why are you telling me all this?

lines.french_street: C'est n'importe quoi, ça me saoule rien que d'entendre ça. T'as rien dit, toi ?
lines.english: That's ridiculous, it's annoying me just hearing it. Did you say anything?

lines.french_street: J'ai essayé de changer de sujet deux ou trois fois, mais il revenait toujours dessus. À un moment j'ai juste dit « ah ouais cool » et j'ai regardé le menu comme si j'avais pas entendu.
lines.english: I tried to change the subject two or three times, but he kept coming back to it. At some point I just said 'oh yeah cool' and stared at the menu like I hadn't heard.

lines.french_street: Haha, classique. Mais attends, t'as dit que c'était un mélange des deux — y'a quand même eu des bons moments ?
lines.english: Haha, classic. But wait, you said it was a mix of both — were there still some good moments?

lines.french_street: Ouais grave, après le dîner on a marché le long du canal et là c'était vraiment bien en fait. Il était drôle, détendu, on a parlé de trucs qu'on aimait tous les deux... j'ai presque oublié le fiasco du restau.
lines.english: Yeah for sure, after dinner we walked along the canal and that was actually really nice. He was funny, relaxed, we talked about stuff we both liked... I almost forgot the restaurant disaster.

lines.french_street: Ah ouais quand même ! Et il ressemble à quoi ? T'as mis aucune photo sur le groupe.
lines.english: Oh nice actually! And what does he look like? You didn't post any photos in the group chat.

lines.french_street: Grand, les cheveux foncés, il a un petit accent du Sud qui est pas désagréable du tout. Mais franchement c'est pas ça le problème, le problème c'est dans sa tête, quoi.
lines.english: Tall, dark hair, he's got a slight Southern accent that's not unpleasant at all. But honestly that's not the issue, the issue is what's going on in his head.

lines.french_street: Ouais j'vois. Et du coup y'a un deuxième date ou t'as décidé de passer ton tour ?
lines.english: Yeah I get it. So is there a second date or have you decided to pass?

lines.french_street: Il m'a envoyé un message ce matin pour dire qu'il avait passé une super soirée et que ça serait cool de se revoir. J'ai pas encore répondu, j'arrive pas à me décider.
lines.english: He sent me a message this morning saying he had a great evening and that it'd be cool to see each other again. I haven't replied yet, I can't make up my mind.

lines.french_street: Écoute, si la balade était bien, ça mérite peut-être une deuxième chance. Mais genre tu lui dis direct que tu veux pas entendre parler de l'ex, hein.
lines.english: Look, if the walk was nice, maybe it deserves a second chance. But like you tell him straight up that you don't want to hear about the ex, okay.

lines.french_street: Ouais t'as raison... bon j'vais lui répondre ce soir. Mais s'il repart sur la Grèce, je me lève et je pars, je te jure.
lines.english: Yeah you're right... okay I'll reply to him tonight. But if he starts on about Greece again, I'm getting up and leaving, I swear.

questions.question: What did the date spend a lot of time talking about during dinner?
What happened after dinner that improved the evening?
options: They went to a bar and had drinks
They went to a cinema nearby
They ran into some mutual friends
They walked along the canal together

questions.question: What did Chloé advise about a possible second date?
options: Ignore his message and move on
Give it another chance but be honest about the ex topic
Ask a friend to come along for support
Wait at least a week before replying

episodes.title: Mon boss me saoule
episodes.topic: Venting about the boss: last-minute meetings, credit-stealing, the coworker who agrees with everything

lines.french_street: Lucas, j'te jure, mon boss me saoule tellement en ce moment, j'sais plus quoi faire. Hier il nous convoque à une réunion à dix-sept heures trente, genre cinq minutes avant la fin de la journée.
lines.english: Lucas, I swear, my boss is driving me absolutely crazy right now, I don't know what to do anymore. Yesterday he calls us into a meeting at five-thirty, like five minutes before the end of the day.

lines.french_street: Attends, à dix-sept heures trente un vendredi ?! C'est pas possible, il fait ça souvent ?
lines.english: Wait, at five-thirty on a Friday?! That's unbelievable, does he do that often?

lines.french_street: Carrément, c'est genre la troisième fois ce mois-ci. Et le pire c'est que la réunion elle sert à rien, on passe une heure à parler de trucs qu'on aurait pu régler en un mail.
lines.english: Totally, it's like the third time this month. And the worst part is the meeting is pointless, we spend an hour talking about stuff we could've sorted in one email.

lines.french_street: N'importe quoi… Et t'as des collègues qui disent quelque chose au moins ?
lines.english: What a joke… And do your colleagues say anything at least?

lines.french_street: Alors ça, c'est l'autre truc qui me rend dingue. Y'a Mathieu, tu vois, il passe son temps à acquiescer, genre « oui tout à fait, très bonne idée », peu importe ce que le boss dit. C'est un lèche-bottes de compétition.
lines.english: Oh that's the other thing that drives me crazy. There's Mathieu, right, he spends his whole time nodding along, like 'yes absolutely, great idea', no matter what the boss says. He's a world-class kiss-ass.

lines.french_street: Ah ouais ce genre de profil… il cherche une promo ou quoi ?
lines.english: Oh yeah that type… is he gunning for a promotion or what?

lines.french_street: Chais pas, mais en plus la semaine dernière mon boss a présenté mon analyse au directeur comme si c'était la sienne. Il a même pas mentionné mon nom, rien.
lines.english: I don't know, but on top of that last week my boss presented my analysis to the director as if it were his own. He didn't even mention my name, nothing.

lines.french_street: Non, il a pas fait ça sérieusement ?! T'étais dans la salle quand il l'a présenté ?
lines.english: No, he seriously didn't?! Were you in the room when he presented it?

lines.french_street: Ouais j'étais là, j'ai rien dit sur le moment parce que j'étais trop sous le choc. Mais là j'en peux plus, ça fait trop.
lines.english: Yeah I was there, I didn't say anything at the time because I was too shocked. But now I've had enough, it's just too much.

lines.french_street: T'as raison, là c'est clairement du vol de crédit. Faut que t'en parles à quelqu'un, les RH ou un responsable au-dessus de lui.
lines.english: You're right, that's clearly credit-stealing. You need to talk to someone about it, HR or a manager above him.

lines.french_street: J'y ai pensé, mais j'ai peur que ça se retourne contre moi. T'sais comment c'est, des fois tu fais une plainte et c'est toi qui passes pour le difficile.
lines.english: I've thought about it, but I'm scared it'll backfire on me. You know how it is, sometimes you make a complaint and you end up looking like the difficult one.

lines.french_street: Je comprends, mais t'as des preuves non ? T'as les mails où c'est toi qui envoies l'analyse ?
lines.english: I get it, but you have proof, right? You've got the emails where you're the one who sent the analysis?

lines.french_street: Ouais ouais, j'ai tout gardé heureusement. Bon ok, j'vais prendre rendez-vous avec les RH la semaine prochaine et j'vais amener les mails.
lines.english: Yeah yeah, I kept everything luckily. Ok fine, I'll make an appointment with HR next week and bring the emails.

lines.french_street: Voilà, c'est la bonne décision. Et franchement si Mathieu continue à se taire, c'est son problème, toi t'as rien à te reprocher.
lines.english: There you go, that's the right call. And honestly if Mathieu keeps staying quiet, that's his problem, you have nothing to blame yourself for.

questions.question: Why is Emma especially upset about last week's meeting with the director?
options: Her boss scheduled it at five-thirty on a Friday
She was not allowed into the meeting room
Mathieu took credit for her work in front of the director
Her boss presented her analysis as his own without crediting her

questions.question: What does Emma decide to do by the end of the conversation?
options: Make an appointment with HR and bring her emails as proof
Ask Mathieu to back her up as a witness
Confront her boss directly in the next meeting
Start looking for a new job immediately

questions.question: How many times this month has Emma's boss called a last-minute end-of-day meeting?

episodes.title: Le mariage de Sophie
episodes.topic: Wedding recap at full speed: the speeches, the cousin who drank too much, the dress, the DJ

lines.french_street: Oh là là Hugo, t'as vu le mariage de Sophie samedi ? J'en reviens pas, franchement c'était ouf.
lines.english: Oh wow Hugo, did you see Sophie's wedding on Saturday? I can't get over it, honestly it was insane.

lines.french_street: Carrément, mais alors le discours du frère… j'suis resté bouche bée. Il a sorti quoi, dix minutes sur l'histoire de leur chien ? Tout le monde savait plus où regarder.
lines.english: Totally, but the brother's speech though… I was left speechless. He went on for what, ten minutes about the story of their dog? Nobody knew where to look.

lines.french_street: Dix minutes c'est gentil, moi j'ai compté, c'était au moins le quart d'heure. Et en plus il bégayait parce qu'il avait déjà bu, c'était gênant à mourir.
lines.english: Ten minutes is being generous, I counted, it was at least fifteen. And on top of that he was stumbling over his words because he'd already been drinking, it was painfully awkward.

lines.french_street: Ah ouais le cousin aussi hein, Théo je crois ? Il avait déjà une bouteille de rosé dans le nez avant même le dîner. Il a renversé son verre sur la robe de la mère de Sophie, genre direct.
lines.english: Oh yeah the cousin too right, Théo I think? He already had a bottle of rosé in him before dinner even started. He knocked his glass over onto Sophie's mum's dress, like straight away.

lines.french_street: N'importe quoi ce mec. Mais sinon la robe de Sophie elle était vraiment trop belle, j'sais pas si t'as vu, elle avait des petits détails brodés dans le dos, c'était magnifique.
lines.english: What an idiot, that guy. But aside from that Sophie's dress was seriously gorgeous, I don't know if you noticed, she had these little embroidered details on the back, it was beautiful.

lines.french_street: Ouais j'ai vu ça, elle était canon. Par contre le DJ par contre… grave la loose. Il a mis du Macarena à minuit, j'te jure j'ai cru rêver.
lines.english: Yeah I saw that, she looked amazing. But the DJ on the other hand… total disaster. He put on the Macarena at midnight, I swear I thought I was dreaming.

lines.french_street: Ça me saoule ce genre de DJ, y'a genre mille chansons dans le monde et il sort le Macarena… Et après il a enchaîné sur du Patrick Sébastien, j'pouvais plus.
lines.english: That kind of DJ drives me crazy, there are like a thousand songs in the world and he pulls out the Macarena… And then he went straight into Patrick Sébastien, I couldn't take it anymore.

lines.french_street: Pfff carrément. Mais bon les mariés ils avaient l'air trop heureux donc chais pas, on va pas faire la fine bouche non plus.
lines.english: Pfff totally. But well the couple looked so happy so I don't know, we can't really complain either.

lines.french_street: C'est vrai ça, Sophie elle pleurait pendant les vœux, c'était vraiment touchant. Et Thomas il lisait son texte et sa voix elle tremblait, j'ai failli pleurer moi aussi.
lines.english: That's true, Sophie was crying during the vows, it was really touching. And Thomas was reading his text and his voice was trembling, I nearly cried too.

lines.french_street: Ouais ça c'était bien. Dis, et Théo à la fin, il est devenu où ? Parce que j'l'ai plus vu après le dessert.
lines.english: Yeah that part was good. Hey, and Théo at the end, what happened to him? Because I didn't see him after dessert.

lines.french_street: On l'a mis dans un taxi vers vingt-deux heures, il tenait plus debout. Sa copine était morte de honte, elle s'excusait auprès de tout le monde.
lines.english: They put him in a taxi around ten o'clock, he couldn't stand up anymore. His girlfriend was dying of embarrassment, she was apologising to everyone.

lines.french_street: Pauvre elle franchement. Bon au moins le repas il était vraiment top, le traiteur il a assuré. Ce magret de canard j'en rêve encore.
lines.english: Poor her honestly. Well at least the food was really great, the caterer delivered. I'm still dreaming about that duck breast.

lines.french_street: Ouais le magret c'était dingue. Bref c'était un mariage mémorable hein, mais pour des raisons très… variées.
lines.english: Yeah the duck was insane. Anyway it was a memorable wedding right, but for very… varied reasons.

lines.french_street: C'est le moins qu'on puisse dire. J'espère que Sophie elle a pas trop vu la galère, elle méritait que ce soit parfait.
lines.english: That's putting it mildly. I hope Sophie didn't see too much of the chaos, she deserved it to be perfect.

questions.question: What did the cousin Théo do that embarrassed everyone during the reception?
options: He gave a fifteen-minute speech about a dog
He fell asleep on the dance floor
He knocked his glass of rosé onto Sophie's mother's dress

questions.question: What did the DJ play at midnight that shocked Manon and Hugo?
What happened to Théo by the end of the evening?
options: He was put in a taxi around 10 p.m. because he couldn't stand up
He was asked to leave by the groom
He drove himself home despite being drunk
He stayed until the very end of the party

episodes.title: Le déménagement
episodes.topic: Moving-day chaos: the van too small, the sofa stuck in the stairwell, the friend who never showed

lines.french_street: Putain Thomas, j'te jure, le déménagement d'hier c'était un désastre total. J'sais même pas par où commencer.
lines.english: Oh my god Thomas, I'm telling you, yesterday's move was a complete disaster. I don't even know where to start.

lines.french_street: Attends, ça s'est pas bien passé ? T'avais pas loué un camion et tout ?
lines.english: Wait, it didn't go well? Hadn't you rented a van and everything?

lines.french_street: Ouais, j'avais loué un camion, sauf que le mec il m'a filé un modèle trop petit. Genre y'avait pas la moitié de mes affaires qui rentraient, c'était n'importe quoi.
lines.english: Yeah, I'd rented a van, except the guy gave me a model that was too small. Like, not even half my stuff could fit in it, it was ridiculous.

lines.french_street: Grave ? Et t'as fait quoi alors, t'as dû faire plusieurs voyages ?
lines.english: Seriously? And what did you do then, you had to make several trips?

lines.french_street: Bah ouais, trois allers-retours avec ce camion de merde. Mais le pire c'est le canapé — on a essayé de le monter dans l'escalier et il a coincé à mi-étage. On a passé genre une heure là-dessus.
lines.english: Well yeah, three round trips with that crappy van. But the worst part was the sofa — we tried to bring it up the stairwell and it got stuck halfway up. We spent like an hour on that.

lines.french_street: Carrément une heure ? Mais c'est qui « on » ? Kévin il était là pour t'aider ?
lines.english: A whole hour? But who's 'we'? Was Kévin there to help you?

lines.french_street: Ah Kévin... il avait promis d'être là à neuf heures. J'l'ai appelé à dix heures, à onze heures — rien. Il s'est pointé à quatorze heures avec un café à la main comme si c'était normal.
lines.english: Oh, Kévin... he'd promised to be there at nine. I called him at ten, at eleven — nothing. He showed up at two in the afternoon with a coffee in his hand like it was completely normal.

lines.french_street: Non, il a pas fait ça. Ça me saoule rien que d'entendre ça. Et il t'a donné une excuse au moins ?
lines.english: No, he didn't do that. Just hearing that drives me crazy. And did he at least give you an excuse?

lines.french_street: Il m'a dit qu'il avait « mal dormi ». J'te jure, j'l'ai regardé et j'ai rien dit parce que j'étais trop fatiguée pour me battre. Mais c'est fini, hein.
lines.english: He told me he'd 'slept badly'. I'm telling you, I just looked at him and said nothing because I was too tired to argue. But we're done, seriously.

lines.french_street: Honnêtement c'est nul de sa part. Et le canapé alors, vous avez réussi à le faire monter à la fin ?
lines.english: Honestly that's really bad of him. And the sofa, did you manage to get it up in the end?

lines.french_street: Au bout d'une heure on a abandonné et on l'a laissé dans la cave pour l'instant. J'sais pas encore comment je vais gérer ça.
lines.english: After an hour we gave up and left it in the basement for now. I still don't know how I'm going to deal with that.

lines.french_street: T'as pensé à démonter les pieds ? Des fois ça change tout avec les escaliers étroits.
lines.english: Did you think about taking off the legs? Sometimes it makes all the difference in narrow stairwells.

lines.french_street: Chais pas pourquoi on a même pas pensé à ça sur le moment. On était tellement à bout que... ouais, j'vais essayer ce week-end.
lines.english: I have no idea why we didn't even think of that at the time. We were so exhausted that... yeah, I'll try that this weekend.

lines.french_street: Si t'as besoin d'un coup de main samedi, hésite pas, moi j'suis dispo. Contrairement à certains.
lines.english: If you need a hand on Saturday, don't hesitate, I'm free. Unlike some people.

lines.french_street: Haha, merci Thomas, vraiment. T'es trop sympa. Au moins toi t'es fiable.
lines.english: Haha, thank you Thomas, genuinely. You're so kind. At least you're reliable.

questions.question: Why did Inès have to make three round trips with the van?
options: She forgot half her boxes at the old apartment
The van broke down twice during the move
The van was too small to fit all her belongings at once
The road was blocked and she had to take a longer route

questions.question: What time did Kévin finally show up?
Where did Inès and her helpers leave the sofa after failing to move it upstairs?

episodes.title: Les urgences
episodes.topic: A (minor) ER story told at full speed: the wait, the triage, the twisted ankle, five hours for a bandage

lines.french_street: Putain Karim, j'ai passé cinq heures aux urgences hier soir pour une cheville tordue, cinq heures !
lines.english: Oh my god Karim, I spent five hours in the ER last night for a twisted ankle, five hours!

lines.french_street: Sérieux ? T'as attendu cinq heures juste pour une cheville ? C'est n'importe quoi...
lines.english: Seriously? You waited five hours just for an ankle? That's insane...

lines.french_street: Ouais carrément, et encore, quand j'suis arrivée y'avait genre vingt personnes avant moi dans la salle d'attente. J'me suis dit ok ça va être long.
lines.english: Yeah totally, and when I got there there were like twenty people ahead of me in the waiting room. I told myself, okay this is gonna take a while.

lines.french_street: T'étais toute seule au moins ou quelqu'un t'avait accompagnée ?
lines.english: Were you alone at least or did someone come with you?

lines.french_street: Toute seule, j'ai appelé ma sœur mais elle pouvait pas se déplacer. Donc j'suis restée là à boiter jusqu'au guichet d'abord pour faire l'admission.
lines.english: All alone, I called my sister but she couldn't make it. So I limped my way to the desk first to do the admission.

lines.french_street: Et le tri, ça s'est passé comment ? Ils t'ont vue rapidement au moins ?
lines.english: And the triage, how did that go? Did they see you quickly at least?

lines.french_street: Pff, une heure pour voir l'infirmière du tri, elle m'a tâté la cheville deux secondes et elle m'a dit c'est probablement pas cassé, allez rasseoir vous.
lines.english: Pfft, one hour to see the triage nurse, she pressed on my ankle for two seconds and told me it's probably not broken, go sit back down.

lines.french_street: Elle t'a dit vous ? Aux urgences ils tutoyent pas ?
lines.english: She said vous to you? Don't they use tu in the ER?

lines.french_street: Nan, là c'était vouvoiement total, t'es plus dans la rue hein. Bref, j'me suis rassise et j'ai attendu encore trois heures avant qu'un médecin me voie vraiment.
lines.english: No, there it was full formal address, you're not on the street anymore. Anyway, I sat back down and waited another three hours before a doctor actually saw me.

lines.french_street: Trois heures de plus... ça me saoule rien que d'imaginer. T'avais même pas de quoi te distraire ?
lines.english: Three more hours... that drives me crazy just imagining it. You didn't even have anything to keep you busy?

lines.french_street: Mon téléphone à quarante pour cent, j'ai regardé le plafond la moitié du temps. Et à côté de moi y'avait un mec qui toussait sans arrêt, j'espère que j'ai rien chopé.
lines.english: My phone at forty percent, I stared at the ceiling half the time. And next to me there was a guy coughing non-stop, I hope I didn't catch anything.

lines.french_street: Grave, t'avais vraiment pas de chance. Et au final le médecin il a fait quoi ?
lines.english: For real, you really were unlucky. And in the end what did the doctor do?

lines.french_street: Il m'a fait une radio, rien de cassé comme prévu, et il m'a mis un bandage. Genre dix minutes de consultation pour cinq heures d'attente, j'hallucine.
lines.english: He did an X-ray, nothing broken as expected, and he put a bandage on it. Like ten minutes of actual care for five hours of waiting, I can't believe it.

lines.french_street: Dix minutes... c'est vraiment le système D. T'aurais pas pu aller chez un médecin de garde plutôt ?
lines.english: Ten minutes... it's really the bare-minimum system. Couldn't you have just gone to an on-call doctor instead?

lines.french_street: Chais pas, j'avais trop peur que ce soit cassé donc j'voulais la radio direct. Mais la prochaine fois j'essaie le médecin de garde, c'est sûr.
lines.english: I don't know, I was too scared it was broken so I wanted the X-ray right away. But next time I'm trying the on-call doctor for sure.

questions.question: How long did Léa wait before the triage nurse saw her?
Why was Léa alone at the ER?
options: It was too late to call anyone

questions.question: What did the doctor's examination reveal?

episodes.title: Retour de vacances
episodes.topic: Back from holiday: lost luggage, the rental that looked nothing like the photos, and still it was great

lines.french_street: Alors, ces vacances ? T'as l'air crevée quand même.
lines.english: So, how were the holidays? You look wrecked, to be honest.

lines.french_street: Crevée, c'est le mot. Déjà à l'aller, la compagnie a perdu ma valise. J'ai attendu genre deux heures à l'aéroport, et au final on m'a dit qu'elle était à Barcelone. Barcelone ! Moi j'allais à Lisbonne.
lines.english: Wrecked is the word. First off, on the way there, the airline lost my suitcase. I waited like two hours at the airport, and in the end they told me it was in Barcelona. Barcelona! And I was going to Lisbon.

lines.french_street: Non, c'est pas vrai. Et t'as récupéré tes affaires quand, du coup ?
lines.english: No way. And when did you actually get your stuff back?

lines.french_street: Trois jours après. Trois jours à porter les mêmes fringues, franchement c'était n'importe quoi. J'ai dû acheter un maillot de bain en urgence parce que le mien était dans la valise évidemment.
lines.english: Three days later. Three days in the same clothes, honestly it was a joke. I had to buy a swimsuit in a rush because mine was obviously in the suitcase.

lines.french_street: Carrément galère. Et la location alors, ça ressemblait aux photos au moins ?
lines.english: Total nightmare. And the rental — did it at least look like the photos?

lines.french_street: Ah non, pas du tout. Sur les photos t'avais genre une belle terrasse avec vue sur la mer. En vrai y'avait une minuscule terrasse qui donnait sur un parking. J'suis restée là à fixer le parking pendant dix jours.
lines.english: Oh no, not at all. In the photos there was like a nice terrace with a sea view. In reality there was a tiny terrace looking onto a car park. I spent ten days staring at a car park.

lines.french_street: Ça me saoule rien que d'entendre ça. T'as pas contacté le proprio ?
lines.english: That drives me mad just hearing it. Didn't you contact the owner?

lines.french_street: Si, j'lui ai envoyé un message le premier jour. Il m'a répondu que les photos étaient 'représentatives', tu vois le délire. J'savais même pas quoi répondre.
lines.english: Yeah, I messaged him on the first day. He replied that the photos were 'representative', can you believe it. I didn't even know what to say back.

lines.french_street: N'importe quoi ce mec. T'as laissé un avis au moins ?
lines.english: What a joke, that guy. Did you at least leave a review?

lines.french_street: Grave, j'ai tout détaillé. La vue parking, la valise, tout. Y'a déjà deux autres personnes qui ont liké mon avis donc j'suis contente.
lines.english: Absolutely, I spelled everything out. The car park view, the suitcase, all of it. Two other people have already liked my review so I'm happy about that.

lines.french_street: Bien fait. Mais attends, t'as quand même dit que c'était bien non, malgré tout ça ?
lines.english: Serves him right. But hang on, you still said it was good, didn't you, despite all that?

lines.french_street: Ouais carrément, parce que la ville elle était dingue. On a mangé des trucs incroyables, on a visité des coins que j'connaissais pas du tout, et le dernier soir on a trouvé un petit resto sur le port, franchement j'en reviens pas encore.
lines.english: Yeah totally, because the city itself was amazing. We ate incredible food, we visited spots I'd never been to, and on the last evening we found a little restaurant by the harbour — honestly I'm still not over it.

lines.french_street: C'est ça les vacances hein, même quand ça part en vrille ça reste des souvenirs.
lines.english: That's holidays for you — even when it goes sideways, they're still memories.

lines.french_street: Exactement. Bon par contre j'vais checker deux fois les avis avant de réserver la prochaine fois, hein.
lines.english: Exactly. Though I'm definitely going to check reviews twice before booking next time, that's for sure.

questions.question: Where did the airline send Emma's suitcase by mistake?
What did the rental terrace actually overlook, instead of the sea?
What did Emma do after her holiday to warn other travellers?
options: She called the platform's customer service
She reported the owner to tourism authorities
She posted a detailed review online
